reviews.text: I was at the Grand Island clinic today.  The nurse was great at drawing my blood for lab work.  However, the provider made me feel like I was a bother and I felt rushed with the conversation.  Maybe it was me but, I sure miss Heidi being my provider there.  The provider that was in from Iowa made me feel heard and took her time to answer my questions.  The one from Omaha today was not a good fit for me.

reviews.name: places/ChIJ21yAh5OHmYcRRTl_-u612N8/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT201b1lYUnhNWGMyV1VoVVJFbHRhVU5QYjFCSmNVRRAB
reviews.text:
Don't trust all of these five stars! They buy them by offering "free B-12 injections".

After my second visit with this clinic I really started to question what was going on and the things that I was told by the providers. I sought second and third opinions from two different, well known medical providers in my area after going to Trilion and both confirmed that no only was this "clinc" just a sales and marketing operation, but their treatment plan was not appropriate. Hormone treatment is based on symptoms not lab results, but I was told I still needed labs drawn every couple of months - doesn't make sense. The med assistant told me that she takes b-12 but doesn't need it so she just "pees it out now" - what medical provider would support that? The self branded supplements were a red flag - pushing things that I didn't need just for their own profit. I never saw medical provider in person, only via telehealth - I had to drive 30 minutes to a clinic to not even see someone in person. Told me to keep taking a medication (that I no longer needed to take) that is known to suppress hormone levels - I suspect so that the labs they insisted on would not have correct results and they could push more treatment.

Also, do not trust their billing plan. Call your insurance company and find out directly what it would cost for the visits, labs and medication - don't just go by what Trillion tells you. Trillion collects from the insurance company and from you each month and ends up making bank while you pay for more than you would if it were a regular billing process. Not to mention that the medications are compounded when they don't need to be and they don't really need to run all of those labs any way. They are way over charging people.

It was very obvious that my treatment plan was not tailored to me and my personal concerns and medical situation. I was given all of the information by the nurse about how to take the medication, how to reorder and what to expect before I even saw a medical provider and got a prescription. They wanted me to take more medication that was not indicated by my labs or symptoms and I said no and was told that we could discuss it again at the next visit.

Over all this was not a great experience. I left feeling gaslighted and continued to question so many of the things that happened in my two visits that I sought out two other doctors and their opinion on my medical situation and Trillion's treatment plan and both disagreed with nearly everything.

reviews.text: U guys do not care. U over charge. Also after quitting u guys. U still charge me monthly. Its going on 2 months now. reviews.name: places/ChIJnYGAVJe9locRGLhpnhc16H4/reviews/ChZDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnTUNRNV8zTldREAE
reviews.text: Where to start..  there is a lack of communication between departmental staff,  use vague non .medical terms, ignore giving out information on costs and insurance coverage, use a "used car salesman" at the end to seal the deal.    I went in and stated what product line I was interested in.   I was told my medical staff that reception staff would provide me that information when I leave.   Had my blood drawn to see my levels.   Since I did not say right away I wanted to sign up for a program. I was walked out with in 15 minutes.   Went  back a week later to go over test results.  I stated with medical staff I never got a insurance and out of pocket cost for the program like you said would be in my booklet when I left the 1st visit.    I was told someone would bring that in but no one ever did on my second visit.  I Was told by medical staff that my insurance might be out of network but will have someone look into it. It could be still in network.   Never got that information and exact dollar amount.   At end of medical visit they sent in a guy named lynn who said he would answer my questions and concerns.   He kept saying he didn't want to be a used car salesman but sure acted like one.   He had no medical knowledge.  Used vague terms like flush toxins.  What toxins was he referring to and how are they being flushed was just one of many things he could answer and would try to change the subject.   When he addressed the concern about out of pocket costs still weren't provided, lynn was shocked reception staff never gave me that info and beating around the bush. He finally stated that insurance would not cover any of that.   He Kept saying. Why aren't you signing up  I would if I was you. Obviously I wasn't going to without more information so his tone quickly changed to escorting me out of the room ASAP.   Weeks later I get a phone call from reception staff about being time to come in for your second blood draw. I guess they must not met quota and needed to pressure someone to sign up

reviews.name: places/ChIJxU9tktiVlocRD0v4f1be2HM/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2tWS1gxaGhjVkZqWDNKT09XNUJSMWhmT1ZWSFNHYxAB
reviews.text:
I wanted to like this place, but I ended up leaving and feeling very disappointed with their prices, their customer service, and their pushing treatments that I didn't want. It became very obvious that it's driven by profit over everything. When I first went there, I was impressed with their attention to me, and with the providers. I agreed to a hormone plan with weekly testosterone shots, although it was $114 a month out of pocket. I am NOT a fan of compounding pharmacies, but thought I would give it a try.

After my second testosterone shot, I started waking up in a rage and would feel rage the whole day . I immediately called them and said that I wanted to stop the shots. They tried to convince me to keep getting them, but at a lower dose. I know my body, and I knew that I didn't want any more shots.

After I cancelled the shots, they lowered my monthly payment...by $10 a month. Meaning that I was paying an outrageous price--$104 a month--for literally half a pill a day of compounded medication that's not covered by insurance. And don't get me started on just how long that medication takes to arrive. I waited something like three weeks.

I finally called Trillion and left a message stating that I wanted to discontinue using their services. Crickets. Nobody bothered to call me back or confirm the cancellation.

Then a couple of weeks later, I got an email stating that they were going to bill my credit card another $104! So I called AGAIN and left a message asking for them to cancel. At this point I asked them to call me back to confirm, since they never bothered the first time I called.

Instead, I got an generic, automated email confirming that the charges would stop. No phone call, nothing. They just drop you on your head when you decide not to use them anymore. I was never rude or demanding, so why the bizarre lack of communication when I stopped going there?

reviews.text:
Experts in Pain Management & Men’s Health — Exceptional Care from Nurse Haleigh, Gracie, and Joelle.

I rarely write long reviews, but the level of professionalism, care, and expertise at this clinic deserves recognition. From the moment I walked in, it was clear that this is not just another medical office—this is a place where the staff genuinely listens, treats you like a whole person, and works with you to actually improve your quality of life.

Their approach to pain management is thoughtful, modern, and individualized. Instead of rushing through appointments or relying on generic, one-size-fits-all solutions, they take the time to understand your medical history, your specific symptoms, and your individual goals. They explain treatment options in a way that makes sense and empowers you to make informed decisions. You can tell they’re experts in their field, and they genuinely care about helping patients achieve long-term relief, not just temporary fixes.

When it comes to men’s health, their knowledge is just as impressive. They don’t shy away from topics that many clinics gloss over or treat like an afterthought. They create a safe, judgment-free environment where you can talk openly and actually get real solutions backed by science.

But what truly sets this place apart is Nurse Haleigh.

Haleigh is, without exaggeration, one of the most attentive, skilled, and compassionate nurses I’ve ever worked with. She combines clinical expertise with a warm, grounded personality that immediately puts you at ease. She listens—really listens—and you can tell she genuinely cares about each patient who walks through the door. She explains every step of a procedure, checks in on how you’re feeling, and never rushes or makes you feel like “just another appointment.”

Her ability to connect on a personal level while maintaining top-tier professionalism is rare. Patients are fortunate to have someone like her involved in their care.

In a healthcare world that often feels rushed, impersonal, or transactional, this clinic—and especially Nurse Haleigh—stands out as the complete opposite. They deliver expertise, empathy, and real results.

Highly recommend to anyone dealing with chronic pain, men’s or women’s health concerns, or simply looking for a clinic where the staff actually cares about improving your life.

reviews.text:
I've been a client of Andrew's for a few years now. In the beginning I had tried about every supplement there was so | was reluctant to try another snake oil. I chatted with him for weeks waiting for the sales pitch. Never happened.
He was always upfront, honest, and never pushed anything on me. I have recommended many friends and they have all said the same thing. He doesn't pitch a supplement that is a one stop solution. He is honest about lifestyle changes and takes the time to understand yours so to tailor a program that isn't generic but specific. Andrew offers what the big clinics don't... compassion and honesty.

reviews.name: places/ChIJ8ahHSQahwKsR8K5UeNPnotQ/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2pWR1RVUjBWREZFZUhsTFduRXhNMjloYmtKQk5XYxAB
reviews.text:
I came to Awakin Men’s Health thinking I just needed my testosterone checked. Andrew took a much more comprehensive approach. He didn’t just look at labs and write a prescription.  He actually evaluated my overall health, symptoms, and lifestyle. After starting treatment for low testosterone, he also recognized signs that something else might be contributing to how I was feeling and helped coordinate a sleep study. That ended up identifying an underlying sleep issue that had been affecting my energy, recovery, and overall health for years.

What stood out most was how thorough and proactive the care was. Instead of a quick fix, the focus was on getting to the root causes and optimizing my health long-term. My energy, sleep quality, and mental clarity have all improved significantly. If you want a clinic that looks at the full picture and not just one lab value, I highly recommend Awakin Men’s Health.

reviews.name: places/ChIJqTSx5D_3k4cRIC5ogt_kb80/reviews/ChdDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSUNRN3BTYXRRRRAB
reviews.text: It has now been 18 months since my stem cell injections, and in all honesty my knees are as painful now as they were prior to the treatment. In addition to my knee injections I also had i.v. treatment, and I do credit these i.v treatments with allevating ankle pain that had troubled me for years. It is for this ankle pain relief that I post three stars. I do not know if it was the stem cells or the experience of the provider, who is no longer with the company,  but I do know that I received no benefit from the injections to my knees. I do still believe in stem cells as an alternative to conventional medicine and am certain that many of you reading this review may find relief through the therapy, simply be aware that it is not always 100% effective.

reviews.name: places/ChIJqTSx5D_3k4cRIC5ogt_kb80/reviews/ChdDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSUM0bFphcThRRRAB
reviews.text: I was so excited and hopeful when I heard about Stem cell therapy. My/our mistake was rushing in the first center that opened in Omaha. I never noticed any improvements from my treatments and my husbands improvements were short lived. My advise to you is 'save your money'. We paid $18,000.00for our treatments and did get NOTHING in return, just EMPTY promises.

reviews.name: places/ChIJqTSx5D_3k4cRIC5ogt_kb80/reviews/ChZDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSUNBaXRidmNREAE
reviews.text: Being forced to give up a very rewarding career as a Registered Nurse really hurt, both physically and mentally/emotionally. When my husband found out about Stem Cell Therapies while researching possible long term treatments for Lumbar Arachnoiditis, Sacro-iliac joint disease, and a host of other musculoskeletal issues, and lung disease it was a possible bright light at the end of too many years of being in pain. With continued research we found ourselves at a seminar to introduce this life returning therapy and then on to the clinic for being treated like royalty wiith ALL questions answered prior to my first treatment. As Dr. Vicki stated, I did not notice any improvements other than an increase in my energy level. After my second treatment I realized that weekend that I no longer had the pain in my Sacro-iliac region or down my leg. "Holy smokes", I have been elated. The staff at the clinic are so awesome they are friendly, very concerned, and continue to answer any questions that we may have. I praise God that we have been introduced to stem cell therapy and the Omaha Clinic.

reviews.name: places/ChIJqTSx5D_3k4cRIC5ogt_kb80/reviews/ChdDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSUNnOUxmM2tRRRAB

reviews.text: Buyer Beware.  These are expensive therapies which to work properly need to be placed in the area of tissue damage.  To achieve this, injections should be performed by properly trained physicians in a setting with the proper imaging equipment.  You risk both your money and getting suboptimal relief by going to a clinic like this.  If you want to ensure your investment in your health, I would seek a trained physician with the proper experience, not a nurse in a pop up shop.

At no point in the now 2 years have I felt like a number or been pressured into additional services. The staff, my goodness... Highest caliber. Even in the cold early mornings I'm greeted, by name, with a warm and welcoming "good morning". From getting my weekly shot, to blood draws and everything in-between the skills of every member of the staff make visits quick and painless. They go out of their way to not only answer my questions but ensure I am educated and comfortable with all procedures being done. At the Papillion NE Limitless Male clinic I'm not a patient, client, or customer...I am just "one of the guys" all free and comfortable to work on and discuss the "dude" things. I am a whole new man since starting and can't imagine life without the support. I feel like I did in my 30s. If you've ever considered testosterone or a safe place to maybe discuss personal Male topics I urge you to give the Papillion NE Limitless Male clinic a call or even just stop by. I wish someone had told me to years ago.

reviews.text: For years I had avoided going to the doctor because I was always told my test results were “normal.” But I knew something had to be wrong. I was able to get in with Charly and knew I was in good hands from the start. She took the time to actually listen, reviewed test results with me so I could understand, and developed a sustainable plan to get me on track and feeling sooo much better. I love that I can reach out and get an answer back right away. So glad to have found this practice and truly feel cared about!

reviews.text:
I love Wellness Redefined and am so glad I made that first appointment  after hearing such good things from friends.
Charly has done amazing things for my body. She has resolved many of my issues that traditional medicine has not been able to. I had been told that I would just need to live with my symptoms because they could not fix them.
I especially love her individualized treatment for me. No "one size fits all" here.
As a member, being able to text her and not always  have to make an in person appointment is important to me.

reviews.text:
Wellness Redefined provides intelligent and thoughtful care.  Charly listens to my  concerns and works with me to come up with a plan that is unique to me, not a one-size-fits-all approach.
I've had some significant health challenges the last few years. Thanks to Charly, I've managed my health in a way that is sustainable for both my family and me.
I definitely think its worth the drive to Seward to see her and I recommend this practice to anyone who asks!
